title: auth — background jobs
description: Eight jobs. Six purge expired/consumed tokens on staggered cadences (per retention.json). One rotates JWT signing keys on a monthly cadence per key. One asynchronously revokes every active session for a User (dispatched by the PasswordChanged listener — the bulk revocation can be slow for cross-Application identities with many User rows).

jobs.purpose: Hard-delete auth_refresh_tokens rows whose consumed_at OR revoked_at OR expires_at is older than 90 days. 90-day tail is for post-incident audit; anything older has no compliance value.
steps: SELECT id FROM auth_refresh_tokens WHERE (consumed_at IS NOT NULL AND consumed_at < now() - INTERVAL '90 days') OR (revoked_at IS NOT NULL AND revoked_at < now() - INTERVAL '90 days') OR (expires_at < now() - INTERVAL '90 days') LIMIT 5000.
DELETE the batch inside a transaction.
Emit `stackra.auth.refresh_tokens.purged` metric.
Recurse if batch was 5000.

jobs.purpose: Hard-delete auth_password_resets rows past their 24h purge window (24h AFTER consumed_at OR expires_at; the extra 24h is for immediate post-consumption audit trace).
steps: SELECT id FROM auth_password_resets WHERE (consumed_at IS NOT NULL AND consumed_at < now() - INTERVAL '24 hours') OR (expires_at < now() - INTERVAL '24 hours') LIMIT 1000.
DELETE the batch.
Recurse if batch was 1000.

jobs.purpose: Hard-delete auth_email_verifications rows past their 30-day purge window.
steps: SELECT id FROM auth_email_verifications WHERE (consumed_at IS NOT NULL AND consumed_at < now() - INTERVAL '30 days') OR (expires_at < now() - INTERVAL '30 days') LIMIT 1000.
DELETE the batch.
Recurse if batch was 1000.

jobs.purpose: Hard-delete auth_mfa_challenges rows past their 1h purge window. Short cadence (every 5 min) because challenges have a 5-min TTL — many rows to reap fast.
steps: SELECT id FROM auth_mfa_challenges WHERE (consumed_at IS NOT NULL AND consumed_at < now() - INTERVAL '1 hour') OR (expires_at < now() - INTERVAL '1 hour') LIMIT 2000.
DELETE the batch.
Recurse if batch was 2000.

jobs.purpose: Hard-delete auth_cross_app_grants rows past their 24h purge window. Fires CrossAppGrantExpired for grants that were never consumed.
steps: SELECT id FROM auth_cross_app_grants WHERE (consumed_at IS NOT NULL AND consumed_at < now() - INTERVAL '24 hours') OR (expires_at < now() - INTERVAL '24 hours') LIMIT 1000.
For each unconsumed expired row: fire CrossAppGrantExpired event.
DELETE the batch.

jobs.purpose: Hard-delete auth_jwt_deny_list rows once the underlying JWT's natural expires_at has passed. After natural expiry, downstream verifiers reject the JWT on their own — no need to keep the deny-list row.
steps: SELECT id FROM auth_jwt_deny_list WHERE expires_at < now() LIMIT 5000.
DELETE the batch.

jobs.purpose: Monthly rotation cadence per Application (or global key). Inserts a new active key, marks the previous key retired_at=now() (still verify-capable for auth.jwt.rotation_grace_period_days). Fires JwtSigningKeyRotated.
steps: Enumerate signing keys per Application (and the global slot).
For each key whose (rotated_at + 30 days) < now(): generate fresh 32-byte secret, envelope-encrypt via KMS, insert new row with fresh kid, mark old row retired_at=now().
Fire JwtSigningKeyRotated per rotation.
Update JWKS cache invalidation for the /.well-known/jwks.json endpoint.

jobs.purpose: Bulk revoke every active PAT for every User referencing an Identity. Dispatched by the PasswordChanged / MfaDisabled / IdentityLocked listeners. Runs async because a User with User rows on many Applications can have dozens of PATs — doing this inside the request would slow the password change.
steps: Enumerate every User row where identity_id = target.
For each User: enumerate active PATs (Sanctum personal_access_tokens).
Revoke each PAT in a batched update inside a transaction (5000 tokens per batch).
Also revoke every active refresh token in the family_id chain for these Users.
Fire AllSessionsRevoked once (with tokens_revoked_count aggregated).
jobs.uniqueVia: identity_id (one bulk-revoke in-flight per Identity at a time)
